children.title: <AXValue 0x7938e92a0> {value = error:-25212 type = kAXValueAXErrorType}
children.description:
i’m debugging claude code hooks with tmux for pane focus control.

goal:
- when a permission request triggers → visually signal + switch focus to the claude pane (after ~0.5s delay)
- when the user approves → immediately restore focus back to the previously active pane

setup:
- using tmux with multiple panes and multiple windows
- claude runs in one pane (“claude pane”)
- user may be in a different pane or even a different window

original working config (simplified behavior):
- PermissionRequest:
  - save current active pane to /tmp using:
    tmux list-panes -F '#{pane_active} #{pane_id}' | awk '/^1/{print $2}'
  - sleep 0.5 (blocking)
  - tmux select-pane -t $TMUX_PANE  (focus claude pane)
  - apply red styling

- PostToolUse:
  - restore focus using:
    tmux select-pane -t $(cat /tmp/claude_prev_pane_$TMUX_PANE)

problem:
- restore feels slow because PostToolUse waits for tool completion

attempted improvement:
- moved restore logic to PreToolUse to make it instant
- made PermissionRequest async (removed blocking sleep using tmux run-shell -b)

issues encountered:
1. async PermissionRequest broke focus switching due to race conditions with claude lifecycle
2. $TMUX_PANE inside hooks is unreliable (sometimes returns values like "121" instead of %pane_id)
3. tmux commands with \; caused parsing issues in hooks (JSON → shell → tmux)
4. tmux select-pane alone does not switch windows → need switch-client when crossing windows
5. pane targeting ambiguity when using only pane_id in multi-window setups
6. hooks operate on "current active pane" unless explicitly targeted
7. SessionStart hook caused errors due to $PWD / quoting issues → removed
8. debugging is hard because hook stderr/stdout is hidden unless using `claude --debug`

key discoveries:
- blocking sleep inside PermissionRequest ensures deterministic ordering (no race with claude)
- async hooks introduce race conditions with claude’s internal focus handling
- tmux select-pane does NOT switch visible window
- must use full target (session:window.pane) OR switch-client first
- file-based state (/tmp) is more reliable than tmux variables in this environment
- hooks may not run with consistent env vars or client context

current plan:
- revert to original working config (blocking PermissionRequest + PostToolUse restore)
- confirm baseline behavior works reliably
- then migrate restore from PostToolUse → PreToolUse WITHOUT breaking focus switching
- keep PermissionRequest synchronous to avoid race conditions

open questions:
- best way to reliably target claude pane (without relying on $TMUX_PANE)
- whether PreToolUse restore needs slight delay to avoid claude overriding focus
- whether switch-client is required for multi-window setups
- how to robustly handle pane targeting across tmux windows
